items.title: Tutoring Services
items.description:
Academic Coaching and Tutoring offers a variety of tutoring services for UD students.
What We Offer:

1:1 Tutoring – Personalized help, scheduled when it works for you.
Drop-In Tutoring – Just show up, no appointment needed.
Study Groups / PASS – Work with classmates, practice together, and deepen your understanding.

items.title: Advanced Degree: Application Form
items.subtitle: For graduate students
items.description: Graduate degrees are awarded in the Fall, Winter, Spring and Summer sessions. Please see the university's academic calendar for submission deadlines for each semester. If applying for reduced credit load, complete this form before the free drop/add period ends. After the signatures are acquired, submit this application along with the payment receipt as an attachment to your program coordinator. Use the form at commerce.cashnet.com/UD-GPE to pay your degree application fee by online check (ACH). (Master's Degree Fee: $50; Doctoral Degree Fee: $95).

items.subtitle: Center for Global Programs & Services
items.description: International students holding an F-1 visa status may be eligible for employment authorization through Optional Practical training (OPT). Eligible students may apply for "pre-completion OPT" (before completion of program) and/or "post-completion OPT" (after completion of program).

items.title: Manage Parent/Guardian Access
items.description: Students can use the Manage Parent/Guardian Access system to grant a parent or guardian permission to access components of his or her UD student record. By granting access to your parent or guardian, you agree to allow the University to disclose some of your FERPA-protected information to the individuals you have identified. (More information is available at www.udel.edu/FERPApolicy.) Students may also use this system to help their parent or guardian with a password reset.

items.subtitle: Student course schedule planning

Course sections in your saved schedules may no longer have open seats at the time of your registration appointment. The planner is available for use for fall and spring semesters only and made available approximately two weeks prior to the opening of registration.

items.title: Math Placement Exam (ALEKS)
items.description: The Math Placement Exam (ALEKS) is a web-based assessment and learning system to ensure an incoming student has the appropriate math background to succeed in his/her math course. The test is required of ALL incoming students, including transfer students, honors students, and students with an AP Calculus score. All Continuing Education (CEND) students wishing to register for a math course at UD, or a course with a math corequisite or prerequisite, must also must take the math placement exam. For more information, see the Math Placement Program Info page.

items.title: Distance Learning Application: UD Online
items.subtitle: Undergraduate admissions
items.description: UD Online brings the University of Delaware to you! Students may use the Distance Learning Application to apply for online degree programs. Course requirements for UD Online/distance learning courses are the same as for on-campus courses. There is no distinction on student transcripts or an earned diploma between on-campus and UD Online courses and degrees, only the delivery format is different. UD Online students access their courses on the Web through an easy-to-use course management system. Courses may include all or some of the following instructional delivery methods: video streamed class lectures, video clips, audio components, guided readings, chatrooms, interactive web activities, and threaded discussions. Instructors are available to assist students according to their course policy either via email, by phone, or in person. Access to email and high-speed Internet is required for all UD Online classes. A small number of courses require scheduled virtual class meetings using a microphone and headset, videoconferencing, or are completely self-directed. A list of online programs is available at the Professional and Continuing Studies (PCS) website. If you have questions regarding technical requirements for a specific course, please write to udonline-tech@udel.edu.

items.title: Library, Museums and Press
items.description: The Library, Museums and Press is a hub for activity, collaboration and concentration. You will find opportunities to discover, consume and create content throughout the organization’s various locations. The main library, Morris Library, sits in the heart of campus on the Green. In addition, there are three branch libraries: the Chemistry Library and the Physics Library are also on the Newark campus, and the Marine Studies Library is on the Lewes campus. Four Special Collections and Museums gallery spaces—including Mechanical Hall Gallery, the Mineralogical Museum in Penny Hall, Old College Gallery and the Special Collections Gallery in Morris Library—display rotating exhibitions that are free and open to the public. The editorial offices of the UD Press, which publishes scholarly works for researchers, are situated in Morris Library. Should you have any questions about the Library or its resources, feel free to ask staff at any service desk. They are your best source for taking full advantage of what the UD Library, Museums and Press has to offer.

items.title: Morris Library Group Study Room Reservation
items.description: The University of Delaware Morris Library Group Study Rooms are intended for academic use by current University of Delaware students. Reservations have priority. Students using a room without a valid reservation must leave when approached by a group that has a valid reservation. No proctoring. Study rooms may not be reserved nor used for proctored exams by anyone. Visibility of the study room space is not to be obstructed. Doors, windows, and panels are not to be covered in a way that blocks visibility into the room, except in the event of a lockdown. Make your reservation online. Reservations can be made up to seven days in advance. Students must log in with a valid UDelNet ID and password. Save the confirmation email. It will be used to settle disputes about who has reserved and has permission to use the room. Reserve a maximum of two hours per day up to seven days in advance. If a group needs to reserve space in multiple rooms, note that reservations are limited to a total of two hours for that day. Please be considerate of other students and reserve no more time than is actually needed. The group study rooms are very popular so please cancel reservations if space will not be needed. To cancel reservations, click the link provided in the room booking confirmation email.

items.title: Annual Program Educational Goal Assessment Report
items.description: Use this form to annually submit information about what students are learning in your academic programs, how you collected that information, and how it is being used. This form can also be submitted multiple times for different programs or groups of programs in the same department or school e.g., one submission for undergraduate programs, another submission for graduate programs. It can be filled out and submitted by any faculty member in your academic unit, allowing flexibility for each unit to delegate this to the most appropriate person or people e.g., undergraduate or graduate program director, associate chair, or committee chair. This form will be routed for approval by your (a) department chair, school director, or program director and (b) dean or associate dean. Additional approvers or viewers can also be added on the second page of the form. Chairs/directors and their deans will be able to use these reports to help guide collaborative discussions and planning each spring. A copy of the completed form will also be sent to the Center for Teaching & Assessment of Learning, Institutional Research & Effectiveness, and Provost's Office to help them understand your program goals and support you throughout your ongoing assessment endeavors. Information and support for this work is available from the Center for Teaching and Assessment of Learning (CTAL).

items.title: Valuable Property Registration
items.description: During the school year, the University of Delaware Department of Public Safety recovers thousands of dollars worth of property. Much of the property cannot be returned as the rightful owner cannot be identified. The Crime Prevention Unit uses voluntary property registration to help solve this problem. The property owner completes an "Operation Identification" registration form with specific information about the property and Public Safety keeps the information on file. If the property is reported stolen the UD Police recommend registering all valuable equipment (computers, stereos, televisions, etc.).

items.title: ScreenU Alcohol Assessment
items.description: Have Questions About Your Use or the Use of a Friend? We believe in providing students with the wellness tools to make healthy choices. By using tools such as ScreenU, students are able to promote their own lifelong wellbeing, while also helping to make our campus safer. ScreenU is an online, anonymous and confidential screening tool that provides students with immediate, personalized feedback about their use of alcohol, marijuana or prescription drugs. Students who take the ScreenU assessment receive strategies to reduce risk and harm related to their use. Students will also receive information about campus resources and services available to support them if they choose to make changes to their use. How Does ScreenU Work? ScreenU works on computers, tablets and smartphones, so you can take it anywhere. It takes approximately 5–10 minutes to complete the online tool depending on your responses. It will assess your current risk level based on your responses to multiple choice questions. ScreenU will also ask you to answer open-ended questions to help you reflect on your use. Remember to answer the questions honestly to receive accurate feedback. ScreenU is free for students, and you can take it as many times as you want! What Will I Learn from ScreenU? You’ll learn some of the effects of high-risk drinking You’ll learn ways to help you or your friends limit risk by making choices that reduce harm You’ll have the chance to evaluate your own decisions. You’ll also have the chance to evaluate your readiness to make changes.

items.title: OISS Terra Dotta Software (TDS) Portal
items.description: Terra Dotta (TDS) is built specifically for international student and scholar services and makes it easier for them to submit forms and maintain their OISS records. Through Terra Dotta, students and scholars are able to officially log on to their individualized profile to find and complete several tasks and applications from one central location. In addition, through TDS, students and scholars will have the opportunity to participate in learning modules to build on their understanding of U.S. academic culture, immigration requirements and much more.

items.title: Internship and Professional Experiences Grant
items.description: The Internship and Professional Experiences Grant is an initiative of the Career Center established to support University of Delaware undergraduate students facing financial barriers to participating in career development opportunities. The grant is intended to provide a funding source for undergraduate students who have accepted (or are working on obtaining) an unpaid or underpaid internship or professional experience where a financial barrier could impact their participation. The UD Internship Grant program may provide funding of up to $5,000 based on estimated need.

items.title: Student Crisis Fund Application
items.description: The University of Delaware’s Student Crisis Fund supports eligible students who face a serious financial hardship due to unanticipated crisis. Situations are considered on a case-by-case basis and may eliminate unforeseen barriers to students’ persistence at UD such as food or home insecurity, travel costs related to a sudden death or illness in the immediate family, out-of-pocket medical costs, or replacement of personal belongings due to fire, natural disaster or theft. View more information and eligibility criteria on the Blue Hen Essentials page.

items.title: IT Payment Form Request
items.description: New to Collecting Revenue Departments who are new to collecting online revenue should first contact the Cashier's Office at (302) 831-1242, or email cashcontrol@udel.edu. The Cashier's Office will explain the procedure, collection options, and fees involved. Please also review the UD CashNet Terms and Conditions document. Service Requests for New Payment Storefronts Treasury Services will review any new payment form requests or requests to change accounting information, even if the client/department has used online payments before. Please download and complete the FY2024 CashNet Questionnaire document. Email the completed document to cashcontrol@udel.edu and krisaber@udel.edu Once Treasury Services approves, the next step is for you to submit the IT Payment Form Request. Service Requests for Existing Payment Storefronts If you are requesting an update to an existing storefront, you may start by submitting the IT Payment Form Request. Please note: A minimum of 3 weeks should be allowed for form development. Updates may take less time, depending on the changes needed. Actual development time depends on the complexity of the request and other projects in queue.

items.title: Student Crisis Fund Application
items.description: The University of Delaware’s Student Crisis Fund supports eligible students who face a serious financial hardship due to unanticipated crisis. Situations are considered on a case-by-case basis and may eliminate unforeseen barriers to students’ persistence at UD such as food or home insecurity, travel costs related to a sudden death or illness in the immediate family, out-of-pocket medical costs, or replacement of personal belongings due to fire, natural disaster or theft. View more information and eligibility criteria on the Blue Hen Essentials page.

items.title: Valuable Property Registration
items.description: During the school year, the University of Delaware Department of Public Safety recovers thousands of dollars worth of property. Much of the property cannot be returned as the rightful owner cannot be identified. The Crime Prevention Unit uses voluntary property registration to help solve this problem. The property owner completes an "Operation Identification" registration form with specific information about the property and Public Safety keeps the information on file. If the property is reported stolen the UD Police recommend registering all valuable equipment (computers, stereos, televisions, etc.).

items.title: Delaware Cooperative Tuition Exchange
items.description: All eligible full-time employees may register for one course each term or semester without payment of course fees at Delaware State University or Delaware Technical and Community College. This benefit covers the cost of academic credit courses of up to four credit hours. A reciprocal arrangement is available to eligible employees of Delaware State and Del Tech for courses at the University of Delaware.

items.title: LinkedIn Learning at UD
items.description: All UD students, faculty and staff now have unlimited access to the rich resources of LinkedIn Learning, an online library featuring scores of instructional videos on topics covering the latest developments in software, creative and business skills. The number of learning opportunities is always growing. Here you’ll find the tools to help you thrive at work, at school and in life. Industry experts will guide you on your learning journey whenever you’re ready to learn and however you wish to learn–on your iPhone, iPad, Android phone, tablet or other mobile device. Here’s where you can develop your skills in Adobe Creative Suite, Microsoft Office, leadership, project management, social media, video production and a wide range of other topics.

items.title: SEMU
items.subtitle: Student Employment Mass Update
items.description: The Student Employment Mass Update system was designed to help Departments manage changes to their Graduate Students and Resident Assistant employment records between semesters. This system facilitates the process of putting them on/off contract, update their funding information or terminating their records without the need for individual JEDs. The system will be available to make these changes during two periods: Summer: system will be available late spring for changes with an effective date of June 1 Fall: system will be available in late summer for changes with an effective date of September 1

items.title: Tuition Exchange Program Application Form
items.description: The Tuition Exchange Program (TEP) is open to full-time employees who have a minimum of two years of eligible University service as of October 31 of the year in which they submit TE applications. This program may be used in conjunction with the University of Delaware's tuition remission program and counts towards the maximum requests for that program.

items.title: Faculty Promotion and Tenure System
items.description: The UD Faculty Promotion and Tenure system incorporates every step of the P&T process while still maintaining a user-friendly interface. The system offers a positive promotion and tenure experience for candidates, reviewers, department and college staff, and members of the University Promotions and Tenure Committee, and provides a single convenient place for P&T faculty candidates to document their achievements. For more information, please contact: facultypromotion@udel.edu Promotion and Tenure Memo Candidate eBinder Preparation Job Aid P&T Faculty Candidate FAQ Document P&T Application: Please read the FAQ Document prior to accessing the application for instructions.

items.title: Tuition Remission Program Form
items.description: The University offers a Tuition Remission Program for eligible full-time and part-time employees. This program allows up to two tuition remission requests per semester for each employee. The student must be either a spouse, same-sex domestic partner or a dependent child of the employee and must be a full- time matriculated undergraduate student.

items.title: DTCC Tuition Waiver Exchange Lottery Application
items.description: This application is for the use of full-time benefit-eligible faculty and staff at the University of Delaware who wish to enter the annual Delaware Technical Community College (DTCC) Tuition Waiver Exchange lottery, as announced to faculty and staff every year in July. This exchange program provides a chance at one of a limited number of scholarships which waive the fall and spring tuition for one dependent who is enrolled on a full-time basis (a minimum of 12 credit hours) in a degree-granting program at DTCC.

items.title: Working Spouse Surcharge Verification
items.description: THIS FORM DOES NOT ENROLL YOUR SPOUSE IN OR TERMINATE YOUR SPOUSE'S COVERAGE UNDER YOUR HEALTH PLAN. FAILURE TO COMPLETE THIS FORM OR PROVIDE SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION WHEN REQUIRED WILL RESULT IN A SURCHARGE BEING APPLIED TO YOUR REQUIRED PREMIUM, WHETHER OR NOT YOUR SPOUSE MEETS THE SURCHARGE CRITERIA. The University of Delaware is committed to maintaining the affordability and sustainability of its healthcare program. To help offset the cost of covering spouses who have access to other employer-sponsored medical plans, UD applies a surcharge to employees who elect to cover a spouse meeting the criteria outlined below. To determine the applicability of the surcharge UD previously required submission of the Online Spousal Coordination Form. This form is now referred to as the Working Spouse Surcharge Verification Form, but it continues to be a requirement for spousal enrollment. To enroll your spouse in coverage, or to keep your spouse covered under your plan, the Working Spouse Surcharge Verification Form must be submitted each year during the annual open enrollment period. Please visit the Working Spouse Surcharge program website.

items.title: ScreenU Alcohol Assessment
items.description: Have Questions About Your Use or the Use of a Friend? We believe in providing students with the wellness tools to make healthy choices. By using tools such as ScreenU, students are able to promote their own lifelong wellbeing, while also helping to make our campus safer. ScreenU is an online, anonymous and confidential screening tool that provides students with immediate, personalized feedback about their use of alcohol, marijuana or prescription drugs. Students who take the ScreenU assessment receive strategies to reduce risk and harm related to their use. Students will also receive information about campus resources and services available to support them if they choose to make changes to their use. How Does ScreenU Work? ScreenU works on computers, tablets and smartphones, so you can take it anywhere. It takes approximately 5–10 minutes to complete the online tool depending on your responses. It will assess your current risk level based on your responses to multiple choice questions. ScreenU will also ask you to answer open-ended questions to help you reflect on your use. Remember to answer the questions honestly to receive accurate feedback. ScreenU is free for students, and you can take it as many times as you want! What Will I Learn from ScreenU? You’ll learn some of the effects of high-risk drinking You’ll learn ways to help you or your friends limit risk by making choices that reduce harm You’ll have the chance to evaluate your own decisions. You’ll also have the chance to evaluate your readiness to make changes.
items.contactInfo:
Office: Student Wellness & Health Promotion
Methods:
Label: (302) 831-3457, URL: tel:302-831-3457
Label: studentwellbeing@udel.edu, URL: mailto:studentwellbeing@udel.edu
Label: https://www.udel.edu/students/health-wellbeing/, URL: https://www.udel.edu/students/health-wellbeing/

items.title: Student Wellbeing Outreach Request Form
items.description: Student Wellbeing offers a variety of programs and outreach services for classrooms, groups and organizations, and residence halls. Programs include peer- or staff-led educational programs, "Ready-to-Go" program kits, event support, resource tables and tours of the Wellbeing Center. Please submit requests one month in advance of your preferred event date.

items.title: Student Crisis Fund Application
items.description: The University of Delaware’s Student Crisis Fund supports eligible students who face a serious financial hardship due to unanticipated crisis. Situations are considered on a case-by-case basis and may eliminate unforeseen barriers to students’ persistence at UD such as food or home insecurity, travel costs related to a sudden death or illness in the immediate family, out-of-pocket medical costs, or replacement of personal belongings due to fire, natural disaster or theft. View more information and eligibility criteria on the Blue Hen Essentials page.

items.title: UD Alert Sign Up
items.description: UD Alert, the University of Delaware's primary emergency notification tool, implemented through the emergency notification vendor, Send Word Now, allows UD to send emergency messages within minutes. UD Alert is activated only during major emergencies, situations where there is an imminent danger to the safety and welfare of students, faculty and staff, or if a state of emergency is declared.

items.title: DELCAT Online Catalog
items.subtitle: DELCAT
items.description: DELCAT is the online catalog of the University of Delaware Library, Museums and Press. Use it to find print books, e-books, movies, articles and more. DELCAT contains records for both UD Library collections and libraries worldwide. Location and availability information for items is provided, and one click lets you view an electronic copy (if available) or request an item from another library. You can also save the record for an item on a list of favorites.

items.title: Hazardous Materials Shipping Request Form
items.description: Complete this form at least one week before shipping hazardous materials. A Material Transfer Agreement (MTA) Link opens in new window. may also be required. Information on MTAs is available from the Research Office. Notify and confirm with the recipient that the material(s) will be shipped and discuss the nature/hazards. Label all sample containers with the following information: Complete descriptions of the material using common name, IUPAC, or other description. Quantity with percentages for mixtures. The words "For Research Only"
